text: 0.3g of fat. cholesterol adds to the tradeoff. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
0.2g of carbs. everything else is fairly underwhelming. in this category, carbs barely matter.
24g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
vitamin b12 at 54% daily value, vitamin d at 0% daily value stand out most. that is only mild support overall.
minerals are basically not part of the case here.

pros first: high satiety protein source because that is a major real-world strength for meat. complete protein delivery is reliable because the category is supposed to do this well. easy to centre a meal around because practical meal value matters.

the drawbacks next: cholesterol headline is hard to ignore because even if the full story is more nuanced, the category score should reflect how extreme the number looks. cost and storage can be a pain because practical downsides still count. brings no fibre support because that leaves a nutrition gap the rest of the meal has to cover.
B tier. Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
